ANONDITA MEDICARE – WEST BENGAL RETAIL EXPANSION

DISTRIBUTION SCALE-UP • Total New Touchpoints: 1,700+ Retail Outlets  
• District-wise Breakdown: • Cooch Behar: 714 touchpoints  
  • Asansol: 616 touchpoints  
  • Hooghly: 411 touchpoints  

STRATEGIC EXECUTION • Market Penetration: Simultaneous expansion across Urban, Semi-urban, and Rural tiers in West Bengal.  
• Visibility: Targeted increase in shelf-space and product availability to capture rising healthcare/FMCG demand.  
• Last-Mile Focus: Strengthening the distribution "pipe" to ensure consistent product flow in historically underserved districts.  

GROWTH ROADMAP • Domestic Strategy: Scaling from regional dominance toward a Pan-India distribution network.  
• Global Ambition: Establishing a robust domestic foundation to eventually fuel international market entry.  
• Sustainable Growth: Focus on deep-rooted distribution rather than just high-level marketing to drive long-term volumes.  

CORE INSIGHT • For a healthcare/FMCG player like Anondita, "Distribution is Destiny." Reaching 1,700+ outlets in just three districts of West Bengal signals an aggressive localized "cluster-bomb" strategy.  
• This granular reach (especially in Cooch Behar and Hooghly) creates a high entry barrier for competitors and ensures better working capital cycles through a wider retailer base.  

Impact: Positive

WEBSOL ENERGY SYSTEM Q4 FY26 RESULTS

FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE (Q4 FY26) • Revenue: ₹401 Cr (↑ 132.1% YoY | ↑ 53.8% QoQ)  
• EBITDA: ₹146 Cr (↑ 86.4% YoY | ↑ 37.4% QoQ)  
• EBITDA Margin: 36.4% (vs 45.4% YoY | 40.8% QoQ)  
• PBT: ₹125 Cr (↑ 89.2% YoY)  
• PAT: ₹125 Cr (↑ 157.9% YoY | ↑ 91.6% QoQ)  
• EPS: ₹2.80 (↑ 140.4% YoY)

FULL YEAR PERFORMANCE (FY26) • Revenue: ₹1,049 Cr (↑ 82.4% YoY)  
• EBITDA: ₹429 Cr (↑ 69.6% YoY)  
• EBITDA Margin: 40.8% (vs 43.9% YoY)  
• PAT: ₹303 Cr (↑ 95.8% YoY)  
• EPS: ₹7.00 (↑ 90.4% YoY)

CAPACITY & OPERATIONAL UPDATES • Technology Upgrade: Converting Mono PERC line to Topcon technology; Overall capacity to reach 1.35 GW.  
• Utilization: Cell Line-2 ramped successfully; Cell utilization at >90%, Module utilization at ~74%.  
• Order Book: Strong visibility with ₹1,161 Cr in unexecuted orders.  
• Balance Sheet: Turned Net Cash Positive with ₹34 Cr net cash (₹152 Cr cash vs ₹118 Cr debt).  

STRATEGIC ROADMAP & OUTLOOK • Future Goal: Build towards a 2 GW integrated cell + module facility.  
• Backward Integration: Management focusing on internalizing more of the value chain to protect margins.  
• Sector Tailwinds: Benefiting from ALMM policies and PLI schemes driving demand for high-efficiency domestic solar products.  

CORE INSIGHT • Websol has successfully transitioned from a turnaround story to a high-execution growth phase, evidenced by record revenue and turning net cash positive.  
• While margins saw a slight YoY contraction (likely due to tech-upgrade transition costs), the 157% PAT growth signals massive operating leverage.  
• The shift to Topcon technology is a crucial competitive move to maintain relevance as the industry moves away from older Mono PERC standards.  

Impact: Highly Positive

NDTV PROFIT EARNINGS WATCHLIST: Q4 FY26

POSITIVE MOMENTUM

• COAL INDIA: PAT ↑ 11% to ₹10,839 Cr. Revenue ↑ 6% to ₹46,490 Cr. Driven by higher FSA realizations and e-auction volumes. Final dividend of ₹5.25/share declared.
• ADANI TOTAL GAS: Revenue ↑ 16% to ₹1,696 Cr. Volumes ↑ 13% YoY despite West Asia geopolitical disruptions. Calibration in passing cost hikes supported volume growth.
• PIRAMAL FINANCE: PAT ↑ 9x to ₹603 Cr (aided by exceptional gains). Total Income ↑ 56.5% to ₹4,783 Cr. Significant turnaround with strong recovery momentum.
• AU SMALL FINANCE: PAT ↑ 65% to ₹832 Cr. GNPA improved to 2.03%. ROA expanded to 1.8%. Strategic shift to secured assets driving profitability.
• CITY UNION BANK: PAT ↑ 25% to ₹360 Cr. GNPA improved to 1.91%. Announced a 1:3 bonus issue and ₹2 dividend/share. Margins at multi-quarter highs.
• PHOENIX MILLS: Strong retail consumption growth continues to drive rental income and occupancy across premium mall assets.
• WEBSOL ENERGY: PAT surges 3x YoY. Revenue consensus at ₹420–480 Cr. Benefiting from massive demand in the solar cell/wafer sector and PLI momentum.
• PRATAAP SNACKS: Turned profitable with PAT of ₹1.1 Cr (vs ₹11.9 Cr loss). EBITDA margin expanded to 4.9% vs 1.2% YoY.
• ASTEC LIFESCIENCES: Net loss narrowed to ₹7.8 Cr (vs ₹16.1 Cr loss YoY). Revenue ↑ 32.7% to ₹159 Cr. EBITDA margins improved to 5.76%.
• ROSSARI BIOTECH: Net Profit ↑ 33% YoY. Strong volume growth in Home, Personal Care, and Performance Chemicals (HPPC) segment.

NEGATIVE MOMENTUM

• JINDAL SAW: PAT ↓ 52% to ₹139.4 Cr. Revenue ↓ 8% to ₹4,634 Cr. EBITDA margins compressed sharply to 10.4% (vs 14.6% YoY) due to higher input costs and lower realizations.

CORE INSIGHT
• Financials (AU SFB, TMB, CUB) and Asset Managers (Nippon) show dominant growth, while manufacturing turnarounds (Prataap, Websol) are gaining traction. 
• Industrial/Pipes (Jindal Saw) face near-term margin pressure from global macro headwinds.

Impact: Mixed to Positive

text:
KALPATARU PROJECTS : SAUDI ARABIA SUBSIDIARY BECOMES WHOLLY OWNED — ALL APPROVALS COMPLETED ✅🇸🇦

• 🏢 Kalpataru Projects Arabia Company (formerly KIOCL) has become a wholly owned subsidiary of KPIL w.e.f. 14th April, 2026 — all Saudi Business Centre & regulatory approvals completed on 26th April, 2026

• 💰 Balance 35% stake acquired from JV partner BIN Omairah Contracting Company for SAR 10 mn (~₹22 cr.) — KPIL's stake rises from 65% to 100%

• ⚠️ Note: KIOCL has negative net worth (SAR -53.94 mn) and reported losses in FY25 (PAT: SAR -22.96 mn) — revenue also declined sharply from FY24's SAR 268 mn to FY25's SAR 90.79 mn

🟡 Impact: Cautiously Neutral (Full control of Saudi EPC arm is strategically positive for KPIL's Middle East ambitions — but the subsidiary's deteriorating financials and revenue decline warrant close monitoring post-consolidation)

• 📋 Application for dispensation of Secured Creditors meeting dismissed — NCLT ordered meeting to proceed as per schedule

🟢 Impact: Positive (Scheme progressing as planned with NCLT granting revised meeting dates. 90.96% creditor consent signals overwhelming support for the restructuring. May 2026 meetings are a critical milestone towards final NCLT sanction — Apollo Healthco demerger & restructuring remains on track)

GOLDMAN SACHS OIL RESEARCH | BRENT TARGET RAISED TO $90 — NET UPSIDE RISKS PERSIST 🛢️📈⚠️

• 🎯 Price upgrade: 2026Q4 Brent/WTI forecast raised to $90/$83 (from $80/$75) — driven by lower Persian Gulf production & slower recovery timeline

• 🌊 Record inventory draw: Persian Gulf production losses of 14.5mb/d driving global oil inventory draws at record 11-12mb/d pace in April — market swings from 1.8mb/d surplus in 2025 to 9.6mb/d deficit in 2026Q2

• ⏳ Recovery timeline: Gulf exports normalisation now expected by end-June (vs mid-May earlier) — 70%/90% production recovery assumed by July/December; 1,830mb of cumulative production losses estimated

• 📉 Softer demand offset: Global oil demand expected to fall 1.7mb/d YoY in Q2 2026 due to higher refined product prices — but extreme inventory draws not sustainable

• 🌍 Supply outside Mideast: Russia (+0.4mb/d) & US (+0.3mb/d) partially offsetting Gulf losses

• ⚡ Three price scenarios for Brent Q4 2026: Benign ~$80 | Adverse ~$100 | Severely adverse ~$120

🔴 Impact for India: Negative (Higher crude at $90+ is a significant headwind for India — impacts OMC margins, widens current account deficit, adds inflationary pressure on fuel, diesel & freight costs. Cement, logistics & petrochemical companies face cost pressure. Watch oil marketing companies & aviation sector)

IMFA (INDIAN METALS & FERRO ALLOYS) – ANALYSIS

CORE BUSINESS MOAT • Integration: One of the few fully integrated ferrochrome players globally.  
• Captive Assets: Owns captive mines and power plants, ensuring total control over production costs.  
• Pricing Power: High operating leverage allows the company to capture maximum gains from surging global ferrochrome prices.  

CAPACITY EXPANSION & SCALE • Recent Acquisition: 150 KTPA ferrochrome plant acquired from Tata Steel (Nov 2025) is now fully operational.  
• Greenfield Capex: 100 KTPA new capacity coming online in FY27.  
• Global Ranking: Total capacity set to cross 500 KTPA (from 284 KTPA), making it the 6th largest producer globally.  

GLOBAL MACRO DYNAMICS • Geopolitical Edge: Chinese competitors are struggling with high shipping costs and supply disruptions due to the Iran conflict.  
• Supply Chain: Competitors rely on South African ore; IMFA’s domestic captive mines provide a significant structural advantage.  

TECHNICAL & FUNDAMENTAL OUTLOOK • Technical Strength: Stock has recently broken out to a new 52-week high with strong momentum.  
• Fundamentals: Solid combination of capacity growth and margin expansion driven by commodity tailwinds.  

CORE INSIGHT • IMFA is entering a super-cycle of growth, where massive capacity doubling is perfectly timed with global supply constraints.  
• Captive power and ore make it one of the lowest-cost producers globally, ensuring high margins even if prices stabilize.  

Impact: Positive

GLOBAL HEALTH (MEDANTA) – INDORE ONCOLOGY ACQUISITION

TRANSACTION OVERVIEW • Target: Asian Institute of Oncology (AIOPL)  
• Asset: 79-bed super-specialty cancer hospital in Indore  
• Deal Value: ₹30 Cr (Cash deal)  
• Funding: Mix of internal accruals and debt  
• Expected Completion: Before September 30, 2026  

STRATEGIC ADJACENCY • Proximity: Located 500 meters from the existing Medanta Indore facility.  
• Synergy: Integration allows for shared operational costs and seamless patient transfers.  
• Market Gap: Specifically targets the oncology segment, strengthening Medanta's tertiary and quaternary care portfolio in the region.  

NETWORK CAPACITY EXPANSION • New Bed Count: Total network capacity increases to 3,658 beds (from 3,579).  
• Geographic Footprint: Continued expansion across Gurugram, Indore, Ranchi, Lucknow, Patna, and Noida.  

FINANCIAL & OPERATIONAL UPSIDE • Capital Efficiency: At ~₹38 Lakhs per bed, this is a highly cost-effective acquisition compared to greenfield costs.  
• Margin Profile: Oncology is a high-margin specialty; proximity to the main hospital ensures a faster ramp-up and higher ROIC.  

CORE INSIGHT • This "bolt-on" acquisition strategy enhances specialty depth in an existing cluster.  
• It leverages the "Medanta" brand in Indore to capture high-intensity oncology cases without the long gestation period of a new build.  

Impact: Positive

Q4 FY26 HIGHLIGHTS  
• PAT: ₹84 crore (+110% YoY)  
• AUM: ₹7,044 crore (+27% YoY)  
• Disbursements: +25% YoY  
• New borrowers added: 70,841  
• Credit cost: 4.3% (down 186 bps YoY; steady reduction over 5 quarters)  
• GNPA: 4.8% (down 17 bps QoQ)  
• NNPA: 1.8% (down 19 bps QoQ)  
• ROA: 4.6%  
• ROE: 16%  
• PCR: 64%  
• Post-IPO equity infusion: ₹710 crore (Feb 2026)

MANAGEMENT NOTES (Q4)  
• Improved profitability and credit quality despite industry headwinds  
• High collection efficiency and disciplined underwriting in micro-enterprise segment  
• Focus on balanced growth with prudent risk management

FY26 CONSOLIDATED  
• PAT: ₹194 crore (+13% YoY) — post impact of new labour rules  
• Disbursements: +20% YoY  
• New borrowers added: 2,06,833  
• Credit cost: 4.76%  
• PAR (Bucket 1): 6.9%  
• ROA: 2.8%  
• ROE: 9.3%

SUMMARY  
• Strong operational growth, materially improved profitability and falling credit costs; continued emphasis on disciplined underwriting and collection.

VARUN BEVERAGES — Q4 FY26 / START TO CY2026 SNAPSHOT

KEY FINANCIALS  
• Revenue (net of excise/GST): ₹6,574.19 Cr (+18.1% YoY)  
• EBITDA: ₹1,528.93 Cr (+21% YoY) — Margin: 23.30% (+55 bps YoY)  
• PAT: ₹878.71 Cr (+20.1% YoY)

VOLUME & MIX  
• Consolidated sales volume: 363.4 Mn cases (+16.3% YoY)  
• India volume +14.4%; International +21.4%  
• Consolidated net realization per case +1.6% YoY; India realization per case -1.5% YoY (pack upsizing, targeted price-point launches)  
• Q1 CY2026 product mix: CSD 73.6%, NCB 7.5%, Packaged Water 18.9%  
• Low/no-sugar mix ~63% of consolidated volumes (Q1 CY2026)

MARGINS & OPERATIONS  
• Gross margin: 55.20% (+62 bps) — aided by early raw-material stocking  
• Recent capacity additions stabilizing; expected efficiency gains

GEO & STRATEGY  
• Growth driven by demand, distribution expansion, capacity additions  
• India: volume-led strategy (pack upsizing, new launches)  
• International: strong scaling in Africa; Twizza (SA) acquired; Crickley Dairy deal agreed (subject to approvals)  
• Interim dividend: ₹0.50/share (~₹169.10 Cr payout)

OUTLOOK / NOTES  
• Confident on long-term growth (demographics, urbanisation, rising consumption)  
• Margins improving despite inflationary backdrop  
• African expansion provides additional long-term optionality

Typical HVDC Capex Cost Break Up

Biggest cost = equipment & infrastructure: Civil works (~20%), valves (~14%), and converter transformers (~16%) together ta
ke the largest share → these are core physical components of HVDC projects.

Mid-level costs (~10% each): Engineering, other equipment, and AC filters each contribute around 10% → these support system design and power quality.

Smaller but essential costs: Control systems (~7%), erection & commissioning (~8%), and freight/insurance (~5%) → needed for setup, operation, and logistics.

AXIS BANK – Q4 FY26 RESULTS & BOARD ACTION

Asset quality & margins

Loan book mix & growth
• Corporate +38% YoY; MSME +24% YoY; Retail +8% YoY  
• LAP & small business banking +16–17% YoY; Home & larger retail single-digit growth

Deposits & funding

text:
INDIA PEAK ELECTRICITY DEMAND – 256.1 GW (25 APR 2026)

• New all-time peak: ~256.1 GW on 25 Apr 2026 (previous ~250 GW hit a day earlier)  
• Driven by an intense heatwave — rapid spike signals rising summer stress on grid and outdoor workers

Demand Outlook
• FY26 peak demand estimate: ~270 GW  
• Structural drivers: urbanization, appliance (AC) penetration, industrial recovery

Cooling penetration supercycle
• AC penetration still low (~10–12%) — heatwaves accelerate adoption

Grid & Capex Implications
• Transmission/distribution bottlenecks exposed during peaks → higher T&D capex, faster approvals  
• Positive for power equipment, EPC, transmission companies

Renewables & Storage
• Evening peaks when solar wanes increase urgency for battery storage and hybrid models

Risks & Watchpoints
• Demand volatility, discom stress, continued coal dependency

Impact: Positive for power capex and renewables/storage demand — near-term grid stress but supports long-term structural electricity investment thesis

- Credit cost 1.9%; beat estimates
- Adjusted PAT beat 20%; beat consensus 4%

MARGINS DOING THE HEAVY LIFTING — NOT DEMAND

• Early reporters: ~14% YoY profit growth vs ~4.5% sales growth — margin expansion, not topline strength  
• Q3 margin expansion: +389 bps; Q4 moderation: +102 bps — earlier efficiency gains now normalizing  
• Lower input costs (milk, edible oil) aided margins; some players saw ~+60 bps operating margin improvement  
• Risk: reversal in commodity cycle could quickly compress margins and earnings  
• Sector divergence widening:
  - Winners: Staples (Nestlé, HUL, Trent) — volume + premiumization + execution; select industrials with operating leverage  
  - Laggards: IT Services — weak demand, cautious guidance; Banks — mixed results (ICICI steady but not broad-based)
• Market behavior: becoming stock-specific rather than sector-wide bullish

What’s concerning:
• Fragile demand
• Earnings quality questions (margin-driven vs volume-driven)
• Potential guidance disappointments

Investor takeaway:
• Short term: earnings-supportive, market may hold up  
• Medium term: without a demand revival, rally lacks durability — watch commodity trends and guidance closely

Sejal Glass Open Offer– Full Turnaround Story

Stock moved from ₹20 to ₹1000 in 5 years.

Started in 1998 as a glass processing company. 

They cut, toughen & decorate glass for buildings. Co grew well, did IPO in 2008. 

Later took big risk by building a raw glass factory, faced heavy losses & debt. Company went into insolvency (CIRP) in 2019.

Old promoters had to exit.  

(2021-22):
Taken over by new promoters from Aarti Industries group – mainly Chandrakant Gogri & family.

They bought control after insolvency, injected fresh money and cleaned up the company. 

Focus changed completely: Only architectural glass processing (no more risky diversions). 

How it Scaled Up Fast:
• Bought a big glass plant in UAE (Ras Al Khaimah) in 2023
• Added more capacity in India by buying another business in 2025
• Raised money from promoters for growth & debt reduction  Now supplying toughened, insulated & laminated glass to real estate projects in India + Middle East.  

Growth Numbers:
Revenue was almost zero after insolvency → jumped to ₹243 Crore in FY25.

Expecting ₹400 Crore+ this year.
UAE business became a big growth driver. 

Margins improved. Order book is strong.  From near bankruptcy to fast growing company in just 3-4 years!   

Key Takeaway:
Good management + right focus + timely expansion = powerful turnaround.

Sun Pharma Acquires Organon & Co.: Investor Call Announced

Deal Snapshot

Sun Pharmaceutical Industries, India's largest pharma company, has scheduled an investor call to discuss its acquisition of Organon & Co. This marks Sun Pharma's most significant inorganic expansion, transforming it into a larger global pharmaceutical entity.

Key Investment Implications

• Growth Catalyst: Enhanced global presence and diversified revenue streams

• Synergy Potential: Expected cost savings and operational efficiencies from combined operations

• Market Leadership: Strengthened competitive positioning in international markets

Critical Watch Points: Integration execution, regulatory approvals, and synergy realization will be key to unlocking shareholder value. Investors should focus on transaction details, financial targets, and management guidance during the upcoming call.

This high-impact strategic move positions Sun Pharma for accelerated long-term growth and enhanced market competitiveness.

Jayaswal Neco — PLI tie-up for specialty steel capacity augmentation
Signed MOU with GoI for PLI Scheme 1.2; committed ₹45.08 Cr capex and phased output growth.

MOU inked Feb 9, 2026: targeted capacity boosts under “Alloy Steel incl. Stainless Steel Rolled-Long Products.”

Key highlights
- ₹45.08 Cr committed capex under PLI Scheme 1.2 (Capacity Augmentation).  
- Phased annual production increases: +18,000 TPA in FY26‑27.  
- +30,000 TPA in FY27‑28; +55,000 TPA in FY28‑29; +80,000 TPA in FY29‑30.  
- Category: Alloy Steel including Stainless Steel Rolled-Long Products.  
- MOU signed with MoS, Government of India, at New Delhi (9 Feb 2026).  
- Participation aligns company with central PLI incentives for specialty steel.

Moat & manufacturing edge
- Focused on specialty/alloy and stainless rolled-long products.  
- Incremental capacity leverages existing steel-making facilities.  
- PLI alignment can improve realizations and capex payback.  
- Scale-up supports downstream product mix and customer stickiness.

Traction & recent actions
- Formal MOU executed to secure PLI benefits and phased expansion.  
- Committed enhancement tied to financial-year milestones (FY26–30).  
- Investment aimed at capacity augmentation, not greenfield expansion.

Growth runway & optionality
- Incremental 183,000 TPA total capacity addition over four years.  
- Potential revenue and margin uplift from specialty/high-value steels.  
- Eligibility for PLI incentives may de-risk payback timelines.  
- Capacity can enable new market segments and OEM linkages.

Interpretation: PLI-backed, staged capex materially de-risks specialty-steel scaling and could enhance revenue and margins.

Read, discuss, or follow for updates on execution and incentive impact.  
Disclaimer: Educational only. Not investment advice.

Raghav Productivity Enhancers Ltd — Niche monopoly, massively underrated silica play
World’s largest silica ramming mass maker with strong margin expansion and R&D optionality.

Ten-year compounder with dramatic margin expansion — a cash-generating base and high-value optionality.

Key highlights
- 32% CAGR — Sales: ₹20 Cr → ₹254 Cr (FY16–26).  
- 32% CAGR — EBITDA: ₹5 Cr → ₹75 Cr (FY16–26).  
- 45% CAGR — PAT: ₹1 Cr → ₹55 Cr (FY16–26).  
- 56% CAGR — Exports: 1 KMT → 80 KMT (FY16–26).  
- 10‑yr avg ROCE 24% | ROE 19% | 10‑yr cumulative CFO ₹167 Cr.  
- PAT margin expanded 5% → 21%; market cap surpassed >₹1,000 Cr.

Moat & manufacturing edge
- Patented indigenously developed process, granted by Government of India.  
- World’s first fully automated silica ramming mass plant.  
- Plant adjacent to world’s densest quartz reservoir — raw material cost edge.  
- DSIR-recognized R&D lab — India’s only govt-approved facility of its kind.

Traction & recent quarters
- FY26 Sales ₹257 Cr (+29% YoY), EBITDA ₹75 Cr (+40% YoY).  
- PAT ₹55 Cr (+48% YoY); ROCE 28%; Q4 utilization ~87%.  
- Exports disrupted by Iran; management reports normalization from April.

Growth runway & optionality
- Domestic share 14% today; target 30% market share.  
- 30% capacity expansion: 414K → 534K MTPA across group.  
- R&D pipeline: artificial marble and semiconductor-grade silica crucibles.  
- Partnerships and scale-up: Foundry push via Capital Refractories UK tie-up.

Strong historical margin expansion plus R&D-driven optionality could materially uplift valuation multiples.

Read, discuss, or follow for deep-dive updates.

Vidya Wires — Ventura Initiating Coverage

**Buy thesis: growth, deleveraging, rerating**

• Rating: **BUY** at CMP ~INR **74**  
• 24‑month TP: **INR 99** (~**35%** upside)  
• FY25–28 revenue CAGR: **~30.5%**  
• FY28E revenue: **~INR 3,303–3,380 cr**  
• FY28E PAT: **~INR 104 cr**  
• RoIC FY28E: **~34%** (high capital efficiency)  
• PEG: **~0.6x** — attractive growth‑valuation mix  
• Capacity doubling to **37,680 MTPA** by FY27  
• Promoter holding **~72.8%**, no pledge  

**Key catalysts / triggers**

• ALCU plant successful commissioning and ramp  
• Strong power & transmission capex recovery  
• Renewable and data‑center demand acceleration  
• Continued margin improvement and EBITDA/ton gains  
• Rapid net‑debt paydown to near **zero** by FY28  

**Primary risks / downside**

• Copper price spikes impair margins materially  
• Supplier concentration (top vendors ~**60%+**)  
• ALCU execution delays or cost overruns  
• High end‑market concentration: Power ~**48%**  
• Historically tight working capital; cash‑flow risk  

Investment view: high growth, rapid deleveraging, asymmetric upside.

Wires & Cables - the important backbone of India’s AI, power & infra boom !!

👉Everyone talks about AI, EVs, renewables, data centres… but very few connect it to the one layer that actually powers everything → wires & cables
👉India’s industry is already ~₹80,000 Cr, and data centres alone can add ₹20-30K Cr demand in the coming years - and that’s just one driver

👉Why this theme is structurally strong?
  🔹Power demand set to 2x by 2047 → massive transmission buildout
  🔹Renewables + grid integration → need for high-voltage infrastructure
  🔹Data centres (1GW → ~8.5GW by 2030) → extremely power-intensive
  🔹Infra + real estate boom → wiring demand everywhere
  🔹HVDC & EHV cables → high-margin, limited competition
  🔹Exports tailwind (China+1 + tariffs)
  🔹Premiumization (FR, ZHF, solar cables) → better margins
  🔹Even copper volatility doesn’t hurt demand much (cables = just ~1-1.5% of project cost)

👉Where the REAL story is?
  🔹Wires (B2C) → low margin, brand-driven
  🔹Cables (B2B) → high margin, project-based, technical entry barriers

👉The biggest shift underway: Companies moving from wires → cables (especially EHV/HVDC). This is where:
  🔹Margins expand
  🔹Valuations re-rate
  🔹Moats strengthen
  
👉Listed beneficiaries to track:
  🔹Polycab India
  🔹KEI Industries
  🔹RR Kabel
  🔹Finolex Cables
  🔹HFCL
  🔹Vidya Wires
  🔹KEC International
  🔹Sterlite Technologies

Emkay on Specialty Chemicals - China trip reaffirms our views on anti-involution

China is increasingly shifting its focus from bulk chemicals to value-added products, which could have meaningful implications for Indian specialty chemical players.

The potential impact spans companies such as SRF, Navin Fluorine, Gujarat Fluorochemicals GFL, PI Industries, Deepak Nitrite, Aarti Industries, Atul, Jubilant Ingrevia, Laxmi Organic Industries, Chemplast Sanmar, UPL, Coromandel International, and Rallis India.

BHARAT DYNAMICS LIMITED – INDIGENOUS TORPEDO MILESTONE 🚀

DELIVERY UPDATE  
• Productionized India’s first indigenous Wire Guided Heavy Weight Torpedo (WGHWT)  
• Delivered to Naval Science and Technological Laboratory (NSTL), Visakhapatnam  
• Delivery date: April 23, 2026

PRODUCT PROFILE  
• Wire Guided Heavy Weight Torpedo (WGHWT) — production-grade system  
• Available in practice & combat configurations

TECHNICAL HIGHLIGHTS  
• Advanced homing and propulsion systems  
• Integrated search, attack & re-attack capabilities  
• Wire-guidance for secure command & control

DEVELOPMENT ECOSYSTEM  
• Developed in collaboration: DRDO (NSTL) + BDL + MSME ecosystem

STRATEGIC CONTEXT  
• Boost to Aatmanirbhar Bharat and self-reliance in advanced naval weapons  
• Enhances Indian Navy anti-submarine and operational capabilities

Impact: Positive — strong defence indigenisation and capability enhancement for the Indian Navy 🟢

BDL — INDIGENOUS WIRE-GUIDED HEAVY WEIGHT TORPEDO (WGHWT) PRODUCTIONIZED 🚀

REGULATORY / DELIVERY UPDATE  
• Production-grade WGHWT delivered to NSTL, Visakhapatnam  
• Delivery date: April 23, 2026  

PRODUCT PROFILE  
• First indigenous Wire-Guided Heavy Weight Torpedo (WGHWT)  
• Productionized by Bharat Dynamics Limited (BDL), Defence PSU  

TECHNICAL HIGHLIGHTS  
• Advanced homing and propulsion systems  
• Integrated search, attack & re-attack capabilities  
• Wire-guidance for secure command and control  

STRATEGIC CONTEXT  
• Supports Aatmanirbhar Bharat / defence self-reliance objectives  
• Enhances Indian Navy operational and anti-submarine warfare (ASW) capability  

DEFENCE IMPACT  
• Major milestone in indigenous naval weapons production  
• Strengthens domestic defence manufacturing ecosystem  
• Potential to reduce import dependence and enable export opportunities  

SIGNIFICANCE: Positive — demonstrates operationalization of advanced indigenous capability, reinforcing national strategic autonomy 🇮🇳

ZYDUS LIFESCIENCES – ANTI-MALARIAL R&D MILESTONE 🧪🌍

REGULATORY UPDATE  
• DCGI approval received for Phase III trials of Zintrodiazine  
• Approval date: April 25, 2026  

DRUG PROFILE  
• Zintrodiazine: novel anti-malarial compound  
• Effective against:  
  • P. falciparum  
  • P. vivax  
  • Drug-resistant strains  

CLINICAL TRIAL DESIGN  
• Trial 1: 651 patients (P. falciparum malaria)  
• Trial 2: 390 patients (P. vivax malaria)  
• Study type: multi-centre, randomized, assessor-blind, active-comparator  
• Objective: evaluate efficacy, safety & tolerability  

STRATEGIC COLLABORATION  
• Developed with Medicines for Malaria Venture (MMV) since 2016  
• Positioned as alternative to artemisinin-based therapies (ACTs)  

GLOBAL RELEVANCE  
• Artemisinin resistance emerging across 8+ African countries  
• Rising need for next-gen anti-malarial therapies  

INDIA CONTEXT  
• ~1.8 lakh malaria cases reported annually  
• Large domestic disease burden  

STRATEGIC SIGNIFICANCE  
• Strengthens Zydus’ innovation-driven pipeline  
• Entry into high-impact global infectious disease space  
• Potential for global health partnerships & funding  

CORE INSIGHT  
• Phase III approval marks critical late-stage milestone  
• Addresses major unmet need amid rising drug resistance  
• Commercial success contingent on trial outcomes  
• High optionality if efficacy proven at scale  

Impact: Positive — strong R&D progress with global opportunity 🌍📈

UPL LIMITED – INVESTMENT UPDATE 🌾📊

INVESTMENT DETAILS  
• UPL Brazil to invest ~USD 86.7 Mn (BRL 450 Mn) in Sinova Inovações Agrícolas S.A.  

SHAREHOLDING CHANGE  
• Stake increases: 49.97% → 55.81%  
• Continues as Associate (no management control)  

PURPOSE OF INVESTMENT  
• Support working capital requirements  
• Reduce debt at Sinova  

STRATEGIC CONTEXT  
• Sinova is a key distribution partner in Brazil  
• Strong presence in Cerrado region (major agri belt)  
• Critical channel for UPL product distribution  

CONCERN AREAS  
• Sharp revenue decline:  
  • USD 849 Mn (Dec’23)  
  • USD 417 Mn (Dec’24)  
  • USD 329 Mn (Dec’25)  
• Indicates weakening business fundamentals  

TRANSACTION NATURE  
• Related party transaction (associate entity)  
• No promoter-level involvement  

CORE INSIGHT  
• Capital infusion aimed at stabilizing a key distribution partner  
• However, increasing exposure to a declining asset raises allocation concerns  
• Recovery in Sinova’s performance becomes critical trigger  
• Risk: capital support without turnaround visibility  

Impact: Negative to Cautious — monitor Sinova recovery & capital allocation discipline ⚠️📉

PINE LABS – ACQUISITION UPDATE 🛍📊

DEAL OVERVIEW  
• Acquisition of Shopflo Technologies Pvt Ltd (100% stake) approved  
• Completion expected within ~3 months  

DEAL VALUE  
• Up to ₹88 Cr (cash consideration in tranches)  

ABOUT SHOPFLO  
• D2C checkout optimization platform  
• Serves 1,000+ brands & 60M+ consumers  

FINANCIAL TRAJECTORY  
• FY23: ₹6.33 Mn  
• FY24: ₹91.58 Mn  
• FY25: ₹147.35 Mn  
• Strong scaling trajectory in a short span  

STRATEGIC RATIONALE  
• Combines Pine Labs’ payments infra + Shopflo checkout tech  
• Builds integrated offline + online commerce ecosystem  
• Enhances merchant conversion and checkout experience  

VALUE CREATION  
• Shopflo improves conversion rates by ~15–20%  
• Strengthens Pine Labs’ positioning in D2C ecosystem  
• Expands full-stack commerce capabilities  

BUSINESS CONTEXT  
• Pine Labs’ online payments revenue already ↑ ~50% YoY (Q3 FY26)  
• Signals strong momentum in digital commerce segment  

CORE INSIGHT  
• Strategic bolt-on acquisition with strong synergy potential  
• Adds high-growth SaaS layer to payments stack  
• Enhances merchant stickiness + monetization potential  
• Positions Pine Labs as end-to-end commerce platform  

Impact: Positive — strong strategic fit with scalable growth opportunity 📈

POLYCAB INDIA – SUBSIDIARY UPDATE (EPC ENTRY) ⚡️🏗

NEW ENTITY  
• Incorporated Wholly-Owned Subsidiary: Polycon Infra Projects Pvt Ltd

BUSINESS SCOPE  
• EPC projects across:

STRATEGIC RATIONALE  
• Forward integration into EPC segment  
• Complements existing cables & wires business  
• Enables participation in large infra projects  
• Expands addressable market in power & telecom  

GROWTH OPPORTUNITY  
• Platform to bid for government & private EPC contracts  
• Leverage existing relationships in power ecosystem  
• Potential cross-selling of cables & solutions  

CURRENT STATUS  
• Newly incorporated entity

CORE INSIGHT  
• Strategic move to move up the value chain  
• From product supplier → solution provider  
• Aligns with India’s transmission & infra capex cycle  
• Optionality play with long-term scalability

**PAYTM – RBI ACTION & BROKERAGE VIEW ⚖️📉**

**RATING & TARGET**  
• Rating: Outperform  
• Target Price: ₹1,500  
• CMP: ₹1,147 → ~31% upside  

**KEY EVENT**  
• RBI cancels Paytm Payments Bank (PPBL) license (effective Apr 24, 2026)  
• PPBL to be wound up → no further banking operations  

**REGULATORY BACKDROP**  
• Ban on new customers since Mar 2022  
• Restrictions on deposits & wallet top-ups (Jan–Feb 2024)  
• Issues flagged: governance, compliance, depositor interest  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  

**NEGATIVE (SENTIMENTAL)**  
• Strong regulatory language remains a concern  
• Incremental negative from perception standpoint  

**NEUTRAL (FINANCIALS)**  
• PPBL already non-operational for ~1 year  
• Business separation already executed  
• Write-offs taken earlier → no fresh hit  

**POTENTIAL UPSIDE (OPTIONALITY)**  
• Scope to apply for:  
  • NBFC license  
  • PPI license  
• Enables:  
  • Wallet expansion  
  • Credit product scaling  

**FINANCIAL TRAJECTORY**  
• EPS: FY25 (₹-10.35) → FY26E ₹9.01 → FY27E ₹22.83  
• Revenue/share: steady growth trajectory  

**VALUATION SNAPSHOT**  
• P/E: 127x (FY26E) → 50x (FY27E)  
• High valuation but backed by earnings inflection  

**STOCK PERFORMANCE**  
• YTD: -11.7%  
• 1Y: +26.7%  
• Underperformance vs benchmark persists  

**CORE INSIGHT**  
• RBI action largely “known negative” → limited incremental financial impact  
• Business now cleaner, with regulatory overhang reducing over time  
• Key re-rating trigger = execution in credit + financial services  

**Impact:** Neutral to slightly positive — sentiment hit, but fundamentals unchanged 📊

INDIAN IT & AI – ADAPTATION PLAYBOOK (AMEYA P VIEW) 🤖📊

CORE THESIS  
• AI = disruption + opportunity for Indian IT  
• Transition phase: survival → adaptation → reinvention  
• Volume-led model breaking, new value pools emerging  

BUSINESS MODEL SHIFT  
• From: Headcount-based (T&M) billing  
• To: Outcome-based pricing (slow transition)  
• Key change: Delivery risk shifts to IT companies  

REALITY CHECK  
• Most “AI deals” still T&M with AI wrapper  
• Label change > structural change (for now)  
• True shift will happen deal-by-deal, not overnight  

KEY CHALLENGES  
• Contracts tied to SLAs (response time, not outcomes)  
• Internal KPIs = utilization, billing/hour → become irrelevant  
• Client readiness low (cannot define outcomes clearly)  
• Large org inertia (pyramid structure)  

ORGANISATION DISRUPTION  
• Pyramid model (junior-heavy) under threat  
• AI replaces entry-level roles first  
• Future issue: pipeline of senior talent breaks  
• Smaller firms (lean/diamond structure) better positioned  

NEW REVENUE POOLS (CRITICAL)  

1. AI INFRA / AI OPS  
• Data centers, AI factories, managed AI services  
• Sticky, infra-led business model  

2. DATA + LLM VALUE CHAIN (BIGGEST OPPORTUNITY)  
• Data labeling & annotation  
• Domain-specific LLM fine-tuning  
• Reinforcement learning workflows  
• Data pipeline preparation  

👉 “Next-gen BPO” → can evolve into high-margin platforms  

WHY INDIA HAS AN EDGE  
• 20+ years of anonymized enterprise data experience  
• Large domain expert workforce  
• Ability to scale human-in-loop AI processes  

GO-TO-MARKET SHIFT  
• Proposal creation → faster (AI-driven)  
• Sales cycle → still relationship-driven  
• Trust + accountability more important in AI era  

AI DEALS – REALITY  
• Partnerships (OpenAI, Anthropic etc.) = learning phase  
• Focus: employee reskilling, experimentation  
• Not immediate revenue drivers  

COST & EXECUTION INSIGHT  
• Token usage, context building, cost control = critical  
• Lack of deep AI understanding → deal losses risk  
• Execution complexity higher than perceived  

DIGITAL SOVEREIGNTY ANGLE  
• AI works well in customer-facing layers  
• Weak fit in core infra (identity, security, integration)  
• Local data + compliance = strategic moat  

STRUCTURAL SHIFT IN INDUSTRY  
• “Volume hiring model” is broken  
• Hiring 50,000 engineers/year → no longer viable  
• New roles emerging:  
  • Prompt engineers  
  • Data annotators  
  • Domain AI specialists  

DOMAIN SPECIALISATION = NEW MOAT  
• Deep industry knowledge > generic coding  
• Regulatory + workflow understanding critical  
• Hard to replicate via generic AI models  

CORE INSIGHT  
• AI expands TAM, but destroys old delivery model  
• Value shifts from execution → judgment + domain expertise  
• Biggest alpha: data + fine-tuning + domain specialization  
• Transition pain real (2–3 years), but long-term opportunity massive  

Impact:  
• Large IT: Transition risk + slower adaptation ⚖️  
• Mid/Small IT: Faster AI adoption → potential winners 🚀  
• Industry: From labor arbitrage → intelligence arbitrage 📊

**NEUTRAL (FINANCIALS)**

• Business separation already executed  
• Write-offs taken earlier → no fresh hit  

**POTENTIAL UPSIDE (OPTIONALITY)**

  • NBFC license  
  • PPI license  
• Enables:  
  • Wallet expansion  
  • Credit product scaling  

**FINANCIAL TRAJECTORY**  
• EPS: FY25 (₹-10.35) → FY26E ₹9.01 → FY27E ₹22.83  
• Revenue/share: steady growth trajectory  

**VALUATION SNAPSHOT**

• High valuation but backed by earnings inflection  

**STOCK PERFORMANCE**  
• YTD: -11.7%  
• 1Y: +26.7%  
• Underperformance vs benchmark persists  

**CORE INSIGHT**  
• RBI action largely “known negative” → limited incremental financial impact  
• Business now cleaner, with regulatory overhang reducing over time  
• Key re-rating trigger = execution in credit + financial services  

**Impact:** Neutral to slightly positive — sentiment hit, but fundamentals unchanged 📊

Robust HVDC Pipeline in the Offing

According to the National Electricity Plan for Transmission, the CEA aims to scale the current HVDC operational capacity from 33.5 GW to 66.75 GW in FY32.

For this purpose,8-9 projects are being planned at a a total cost of ~ Rs 1,90,000 crore till FY32. Going beyond FY32, additional 6-7 projects are being planned with a Rs 1,00,000 crore investment.

HVDC theme is not a 1-2 year theme. Doesn't mean one should chase at any price. There will be intermittent corrections and that might be the opportunity for those who are not playing the theme.

Information credits: IDBI Capital

**HAVELLS – BROKERAGE VIEWS**

• Motilal Oswal Financial Services: Neutral rating; target price ₹1,349
• Nomura: Buy rating; target price ₹1,620
• HSBC: Buy rating; target price cut to ₹1,560
• CLSA: Outperform; target price ₹1,535

Impact: Positive – majority Buy/Outperform views indicate confidence in growth, though some valuation concerns persist

**MOSL ON HAVELLS – NEUTRAL VIEW ⚡️**

• Rating: Neutral  
• Target Price: ₹1,349  

**Q4 PERFORMANCE**  
• C&W segment strong  
• ECD, Lloyd remain weak  

**MARGIN DRIVERS**  
• Inventory gains supported margins  
• Year-end adjustments aided recovery  
• Lighting, C&W benefited  

**DEMAND TRENDS**  
• Industrial, infra demand strong  
• Consumer segments subdued  
• Cost pressures persist  

**COOLING SEGMENT**  
• Demand picking up recently  
• Summer revival expected  

**MANAGEMENT VIEW**  
• No growth guidance  
• Macro uncertainty persists  

**FOCUS AREAS**  
• Efficiency improvement  
• Brand building  
• Innovation push  
• Distribution expansion  

**CORE INSIGHT**  
• Infra-led strength vs consumer weakness  
• Recovery dependent on demand revival  

**Impact:** Neutral

**NOMURA ON HAVELLS – BUY VIEW ⚡️**

• Rating: Buy  
• Target Price: ₹1,620  

**Q4 PERFORMANCE**  
• Results ahead of estimates  
• Supported by select segments  

**DEMAND OUTLOOK**  
• Consumption recovery key catalyst  
• Normal summer to aid growth  

**MARGIN OUTLOOK**  
• Gradual improvement expected  
• Cost pressures remain  

**ESTIMATE REVISION**  
• Revenue cut: ~4%  
• EBITDA margin: ↓ to 10.7% / 11.4%  
• EPS cut: ~11% / 6%  

**CORE INSIGHT**  
• Recovery-led story intact  
• Near-term pressure, long-term positive  

**Impact:** Positive

**HSBC ON HAVELLS – BUY (TP CUT) ⚡️**

• Rating: Buy  
• Target Price: ₹1,560 (cut)  

**Q4 PERFORMANCE**  
• Revenue: ↑ 2% YoY  
• Overall unimpressive quarter  
• Lloyd revenue: ↓ 19% YoY  

**SEGMENT TRENDS**  
• Cables & Wires strong  
• Solar segment strong  
• Healthy contribution margins  

**ESTIMATE REVISION**  
• Cuts in ECD segment  
• Cuts in Lloyd segment  

**CORE INSIGHT**  
• Weak consumer-facing segments  
• Infra segments supporting growth  

**Impact:** Neutral to Positive

**CLSA ON HAVELLS – OUTPERFORM VIEW ⚡️**

• Rating: Outperform  
• Target Price: ₹1,535  

**Q4 PERFORMANCE**  
• EBITDA: ↓ 6% YoY (below estimates)  
• Revenue: ↑ 2% YoY  

**SEGMENT TRENDS**  
• Cables & wires strong (commodity impact)  
• Renewables (solar) positive  

**WEAKNESS AREAS**  
• Cooling products impacted  
• Unseasonal rains effect  
• Slow summer onset  
• Pre-buying impact  

**OUTLOOK**  
• Low base supports Q1 FY27  
• Harsher summer positive  
• Demand recovery expected  

**RE-RATING TRIGGER**  
• Earnings upgrades key  
• Consensus revisions needed  

**CORE INSIGHT**  
• Short-term weakness  
• Recovery dependent on summer demand  

**Impact:** Neutral to Positive
